results.title: Marriage for moderns: In time of trouble
results.creator_credit: Willard Van Dyke and Affiliated Film Producers
results.brief_description: Dramatizes the experiences of a couple whose happy marriage is jeopardized by a selfish lack of understanding of the partnership aspects of married life. When Mrs Denkman comes for counsel to Dr Burns, the clergyman who married her, she takes a wise step. Dr Burns is able to advise her on remedial measures for her marriage, which is declining because of problems revealed in her husband’s heavy drinking.

results.title: Marriage and divorce
results.brief_description: No ceremony symbolises the hopes of man and woman than that or marriage. Every day is someone’s wedding day. Yet in a world full of disturbing realities, one out of every three couples (in America) is heading for trouble. This film surveys the problem of broken homes and offers the opinions of experts as to what should be done.

results.title: Marlon Brando: 'the wild one'
results.brief_description: After electrifying New York audiences with his stage performances, Marlon Brando excited - even offended the world in the screen version of “A Streetcar Named Desire” - instantly becoming a Hollywood legend, later accorded the greatest actor of his time. But the image that theatre and cinema made of him did not coincide in anyway with Brando the man. Capture the real Brando in this expose of the significant events that shaped “the wild one’s” life including interviews with his family, friends and fellow Hollywood stars (Anthony Quinn, Jane Fonda, Susannah York…). Plus rare footage from his social life as well as highlights of all his legendary screen performances.

results.title: Marlin Waters
results.brief_description: Deep sea fishing off the coast of New Zealand. Photographed largely around the Bay of Islands, which has become a centre for the sport and attracts big game fishermen from the United States, who pass on their skill to newcomers. Shows several long fights with the big fish.

results.title: Marketing wool (Wool marketing in the 1950s)
results.creator_credit: Frank Land and Australian Wool Corporation
results.brief_description: Shows the various phases in handling wool from the time it is pressed into bales at the shearing shed until it is dispatched overseas. The wool auction system is illustrated and the point made that wool growers do not have any control over the price their wool brings at auction.

results.title: Marketing is the link
results.brief_description: SUMMARY: The philosophy and principles of marketing are expressed by 3 businessmen who outline the way in which they entered the field and expanded their activities

results.title: Market town
results.brief_description: A film about market day in an old Nottinghamshire town. The roads leading to the town are full of traffic, for the country people are bringing their cattle, poultry, fruit and vegetables and other farm produce to market. At the market, farmers show each other sample of grain, crowd the cattle market, and sit about talking of the weather and the prices of the crops - the subject farmers everywhere talk about.

results.title: Mark Twain: background for his works
results.brief_description: Using authentic locations, quotations, and re-enactments, this biography provides a background to the study of the work of Mark Twain. The variety and color of Twain’s boyhood on the Mississippi are shown to be a source of his major writings; his other works, as well, are seen to reflect the rich and zestful life of this journalist, yarnspinner and humourist.

results.title: Mark Twain gives an interview
results.brief_description: Hal Holbrook’s clever impersonation of Mark Twain is shown in this recreation of an interview with the American author. Holbrook’s amazing physical resemblance to Twain and his skill in reproducing the author’s mannerisms are combined with the witty and pungent dialogue which characterised Twain’s writing.

results.title: Mark Twain's America
results.creator_credit: Donald B. Hyatt and National Broadcasting Company
results.brief_description: A record of Mark Twain’s America, using contemporary engravings and photographs. Mark Twain as the storyteller describes the atmosphere as he saw American history during his lifetime. The narration is taken in part from the writings of Twain and his contemporaries. Originally televised in the series “Project 20”, this is one of the longest films yet made based entirely on still photographs and prints. Credits include script by Richard Hanser, music by Robert Russell Bennett, narrated by Howard Lindsay and edited by Silvio D’Alisera.
